title: BEFORE INSERT trigger calls helper that REVOKEd EXECUTE from service_role — direct match_decisions INSERTs will fail with 42501
summary: _match_decisions_visibility_check() (the BEFORE-INSERT trigger function) is NOT marked SECURITY DEFINER and runs as the caller's role. It invokes public._assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(uuid,uuid). Line 104 revokes ALL EXECUTE on the helper FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated, AND service_role. Postgres checks EXECUTE at call time even for SECURITY DEFINER targets — so when an authenticated/service_role client INSERTs into match_decisions outside the commit_scenario_batch RPC, the trigger fires, the trigger function (as service_role) calls the helper, and Postgres raises 42501 permission denied for function _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator. This kills (a) src/app/api/match/decisions/holding/route.ts (admin.from('match_decisions').insert(... kind='bridge_recommended')), (b) src/app/api/admin/match/decisions/route.ts (admin INSERT bridge_recommended), and (c) every CI test that does admin.from('match_decisions').insert(...) for bridge_recommended/voluntary_add (T_INSERT_BR in src/__tests__/match-decisions-schema.test.ts, src/__tests__/match-decisions-xor-rls.test.ts, src/__tests__/bridge-outcomes-voluntary-schema.test.ts). commit_scenario_batch path survives because that function is SECURITY DEFINER (owned by postgres → ACL-bypass), but every direct service_role INSERT path is broken.
evidence: L41-L102 declares _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator AS LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER. L104: REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public._assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(UUID, UUID) FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated, service_role; L114-L134 declares _match_decisions_visibility_check() AS LANGUAGE plpgsql (no SECURITY DEFINER); L125 calls public._assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(NEW.strategy_id, NEW.allocator_id). L146-L149 creates the BEFORE-INSERT trigger. src/app/api/match/decisions/holding/route.ts L120-L133 does admin.from('match_decisions').insert({... kind: 'bridge_recommended' ...}) — straight service_role INSERT, no DEFINER wrapper. src/app/api/admin/match/decisions/route.ts L61-L73 same pattern.
fix: Pick ONE: (1) Drop `service_role` from the REVOKE on L104 and add `GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public._assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(UUID, UUID) TO service_role, authenticated;` (consistent with how sanitize_user/commit_scenario_batch are granted to service_role on L232-L233 of 20260516160100). The helper is STABLE + read-only — no security gain from REVOKEing from app roles. (2) Add `SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_catalog` to _match_decisions_visibility_check() so the trigger runs as the function owner (postgres) and trivially holds EXECUTE on the helper. Option 1 is the standard convention used elsewhere in this PR (see 20260516160100 L232-L233).

title: New v2 CHECK constraints lack a VALIDATE step — operator runbook required but no automation/issue created
summary: 20260516160400 (bridge_recommended XOR) and 20260516160500 (voluntary_modify) both ADD CONSTRAINT ... NOT VALID and rely on a hand-written runbook (NOTICE message) to tell the operator to run ALTER TABLE ... VALIDATE CONSTRAINT after backfill. There is no automation, no follow-up migration, and no GH issue/PR scheduled to validate. The NOT VALID state means new rows are gated but the *enforced-on-full-table* invariant is never reached unless an operator manually executes the documented step. If the operator misses the NOTICE (cron.job_run_details is rarely scraped, see L25-L29 of 20260516160200), the v2 CHECKs remain forever NOT VALID. Existing rows that violate the invariant are not flagged in any health check.
evidence: L88-L102 of 160400 + L65-L80 of 160500: ADD CONSTRAINT ... NOT VALID with a RAISE NOTICE that instructs an operator to run ALTER TABLE match_decisions VALIDATE CONSTRAINT ... manually. No equivalent migration or scheduled job in supabase/migrations/ does the VALIDATE. grep -rn 'VALIDATE CONSTRAINT.*match_decisions_kind' supabase/ returns only the NOTICE strings.
fix: Either: (a) follow-up migration 20260516160900_match_decisions_constraint_validate.sql that runs ALTER TABLE match_decisions VALIDATE CONSTRAINT match_decisions_kind_bridge_recommended_v2 + ALTER TABLE match_decisions VALIDATE CONSTRAINT match_decisions_kind_voluntary_modify_v2 (wrapped in a DO block that catches check_violation and surfaces the violator count), OR (b) add a violator-count probe to the next health-check cron that emits an audit_log row when v_both_set > 0 / v_orphan_count > 0. Without either, the CHECKs are effectively per-insert filters with no full-table guarantee — undermining the H-0956/H-0957/H-0962/H-0963 closures.

title: _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator returns TRUE on orphan-org (zero members) — privacy/visibility regression mid-sanitize
summary: L82-L84: if the strategy's owning organization has zero members (post-sanitize orphan state), the helper returns TRUE — making the strategy globally visible to ANY allocator. This is documented as intentional ('avoid frozen scenario commits when the owning org was post-sanitize emptied'). Two concerns: (1) Race window — sanitize_user (mig 160100 L172) DELETEs the user from organization_members BEFORE auth.users UPDATE. During this window, an allocator who already had the strategy in their scenario can commit it. (2) The orphan-org path is indistinguishable from a freshly-created org that hasn't onboarded yet — those strategies should NOT be globally visible. The mitigation undercuts the very visibility gate it's supposed to enforce.
evidence: L78-L84: SELECT COUNT(*) ... FROM organization_members WHERE organization_id = v_org_id; IF v_org_member_count = 0 THEN RETURN TRUE. sanitize_user mig 160100 L172 (DELETE FROM organization_members WHERE user_id = p_user_id) runs inside the sanitize tx with an advisory lock — a sole-admin sanitize empties the org before the auth.users UPDATE on L185. M-0825 also notes (L19-L22): 'If the visibility predicate is meant to gate access, returning TRUE for orphan orgs creates a back-door for strategies whose org was emptied legitimately or maliciously.'
fix: Tighten the orphan-org branch: only return TRUE when strategies.status = 'published' AND a sentinel marker (e.g., organizations.archived_at IS NULL) confirms the org was archived deliberately. Or fail closed (return FALSE) and add an admin-runbook + audit_log emission for the rare case where a post-sanitize commit needs explicit override. Lower-effort interim: emit a NOTICE / audit_log row each time the orphan-org branch is taken so the path is observable.

title: No tests cover 20260516160700 visibility-check trigger — regression risk on every future match_decisions touch
summary: grep -rn 'visibility_check|_assert_strategy_visible' tests/ src/__tests__/ src/app/ returns zero hits. The new trigger gates voluntary_add + bridge_recommended INSERTs but no test asserts (a) trigger fires on bridge_recommended INSERT when allocator-not-in-org → 42501, (b) trigger no-ops on voluntary_remove/voluntary_modify, (c) trigger respects orphan-org branch, (d) trigger does NOT block commit_scenario_batch path (which it works by ACL-bypass through SECURITY DEFINER). Without these tests, the critical finding above (REVOKE-blocks-trigger) would not be caught by CI, and any future regression on the helper signature or trigger predicate would land silently.
evidence: grep -rn '_assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator|_match_decisions_visibility_check|match_decisions_visibility_check' src/__tests__/ tests/ returns nothing. src/__tests__/match-decisions-schema.test.ts T_INSERT_BR (L313-L335 region) does admin.from('match_decisions').insert({... kind: 'bridge_recommended' ...}) — this test would actually FAIL with 42501 if the migrations land as-is (see critical finding).
fix: Add src/__tests__/match-decisions-visibility-trigger.test.ts with at minimum: (a) HAPPY: admin INSERT bridge_recommended where allocator IS in the strategy's org → success, (b) HAPPY: admin INSERT bridge_recommended where strategy.organization_id IS NULL → success, (c) DENY: admin INSERT bridge_recommended where allocator NOT in the strategy's org → 42501, (d) NO-OP: admin INSERT voluntary_modify/voluntary_remove regardless of org membership → success, (e) PASS-THROUGH: commit_scenario_batch RPC succeeds even when trigger is in place (proves SECURITY DEFINER path works). Without (c), the ACL trap in the critical finding is invisible until prod.

title: 160400 + 160500 + 160600 are inter-dependent but each commits independently — partial-apply leaves match_decisions in an intermediate state
summary: 160400 (bridge_recommended XOR), 160500 (voluntary_modify NULL), 160600 (drop kind DEFAULT), 160700 (visibility trigger), and 160800 (diff validator) are logically a single atomic mitigation slice — each migration depends on the others for the overall H-095x/M-082x closures. But each migration commits independently. If 160400 lands but 160500 fails (e.g., violator-count exception, lock_timeout fires), match_decisions has the XOR-tightened bridge_recommended CHECK but the OLD voluntary_modify CHECK — the cron mis-attribution path described in H-0961 is partially closed (mid-state). Worse, if 160700 fails AFTER 160400+160500 succeed, the v2 CHECKs are live but no visibility trigger — every direct service_role INSERT into match_decisions still bypasses the org-visibility predicate (the original M-0825 vulnerability is unmitigated despite v2 CHECKs lulling reviewers into 'closed' state).
evidence: Each of 160400, 160500, 160600, 160700, 160800 has BEGIN ... COMMIT at file scope. Supabase's migration runner applies each as a separate transaction. There is no schema_migrations entry that gates 160500 on 160400 success. If 160400 partial-applies (e.g., the verifying DO block on L117-L141 raises), 160500-160800 still attempt to run when the migration runner is invoked again — they'll see v_v1_present FALSE for bridge_recommended (since v1 was dropped) and the v2 was never added.
fix: Either (a) merge 160400+160500+160600+160700 into one single migration file with one BEGIN/COMMIT so all five mutations apply atomically, OR (b) add a 'transaction-block precondition' DO block at the top of 160500/160700 that asserts the prior migration's invariants (e.g., 160500's preflight should check match_decisions_kind_bridge_recommended_v2 exists), aborting cleanly if 160400 didn't fully apply. Option (a) is safer; (b) preserves the per-file granularity at the cost of more guard code.

title: SET lock_timeout uses session-style SET, not SET LOCAL — works in BEGIN but `SET LOCAL` is the conventional safer form
summary: All 9 new migrations use `SET lock_timeout = '5s'` (without LOCAL) inside a BEGIN/COMMIT block. Postgres SET inside a transaction is reset on COMMIT for session-pooled connections only by certain pooler configs — Supabase's PgBouncer in transaction mode resets on transaction boundary, but for direct connections the SET would leak across transactions if the migration runner reuses the session. `SET LOCAL lock_timeout = '5s'` would be the standard safer pattern (resets at COMMIT regardless of pooling). The functional impact is low because Supabase's migration runner typically uses a fresh transaction per file, but the convention here drifts from PG best practice and from the project's own pattern elsewhere.
evidence: All 9 files L21/L23/L25/L28/L29/L31/L33/L36/L42: SET lock_timeout = '5s'; (no LOCAL). Postgres docs (https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/sql-set.html): 'The effect of SET LOCAL is reset by the end of the transaction. SET (without LOCAL) is reset at end of session, or by an unsuccessful transaction abort.' For migrations that need the timeout to be scoped to ONE transaction, SET LOCAL is the documented form.
fix: s/SET lock_timeout = '5s';/SET LOCAL lock_timeout = '5s';/g across all 9 files. No behavioral change in normal apply; defensive correctness only. Could be a follow-up commit rather than blocking this PR.

title: 160300 STEP 1 bootstrap CREATE TYPE branch references a kind column that won't exist on a fresh DB without mig 080
summary: STEP 1 says 'On a fresh DB where mig 080 has not yet applied (e.g., sibling branch or recovery snapshot), CREATE TYPE.' But if mig 080 hasn't applied, match_decisions.kind column doesn't exist either, and 160400/160500/160600 will fail referencing match_decisions.kind. The bootstrap branch is half-complete: it creates the type but not the column. In practice the timestamp ordering guarantees mig 080 (20260426131718) runs before 160300, so this branch never fires on the canonical chain — but the comment 'sibling branch or recovery snapshot' implies someone DID intend to support out-of-order replay, and the bootstrap is incomplete for that case.
evidence: L44-L58 conditionally creates the enum if absent. No equivalent guard for match_decisions.kind column existence. mig 080 L82-L84 (ALTER TABLE match_decisions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS kind match_decision_kind) — this is the only place the column is added. If a recovery snapshot is from pre-mig-080 state and 160300 is replayed, the bootstrap creates the type but no column → 160400 fails at ADD CONSTRAINT match_decisions_kind_bridge_recommended_v2 CHECK (kind <> ... with 42703 undefined_column.
fix: Either: (a) drop the STEP 1 bootstrap entirely (rely strictly on timestamp ordering / mig 080 dependency) and surface the missing-column case in the assertion at STEP 2, OR (b) extend STEP 1 to also ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS kind match_decision_kind. (a) is simpler and matches reality; (b) makes the migration fully self-contained for fresh apply. The comment on L44 should be deleted if (a) is chosen, since the 'sibling branch' use case is unsupported anyway.

title: 160100 sanitize_user DELETE FROM notification_dispatches is filtered ONLY by recipient_email — leaks subjects/metadata for sole-recipient sends to NULL email
summary: L148-L159: the DELETE FROM notification_dispatches WHERE recipient_email = v_target_email only fires if v_target_email IS NOT NULL. v_target_email is captured on L73 from profiles.email — but if the user's profiles.email was already NULL (e.g., a second sanitize_user call, or a partial prior sanitize), v_target_email is NULL and the DELETE is skipped entirely. notification_dispatches rows for this user may persist (subject + metadata may contain PII like first name in welcome emails). This is a GDPR Art. 17 gap: the second-sanitize / partial-state path leaves PII forever.
evidence: L73: SELECT email INTO v_target_email FROM profiles WHERE id = p_user_id; L148: IF v_target_email IS NOT NULL THEN ... DELETE FROM notification_dispatches WHERE recipient_email = v_target_email; END IF;. notification_dispatches.subject + notification_dispatches.metadata (mig 20260409002118 L19, L22) are not constrained to PII-free content. mig 080 'already_sanitized' branch (L62-L71) short-circuits when display_name = '[deleted]' — but if a prior sanitize set display_name to '[deleted]' but errored before completing the email purge, re-running sanitize_user returns FALSE on L70 without ever cleaning notification_dispatches.
fix: Move the DELETE outside the IF v_target_email IS NOT NULL block. Either (a) keep a parallel lookup by metadata->>'user_id' if/when that field is populated, OR (b) probe notification_dispatches for rows where metadata->>'user_id' = p_user_id::text AND recipient_email IS NULL (post-prior-purge state) and purge those too. As a minimum: emit a NOTICE listing notification_dispatches rows whose recipient_email matches a known-deleted-user pattern so admins can manually clean.
